title: Circus parable — 18-year-old who 'imitates birds' and flies away
html:
Circus parable — 18-year-old who 'imitates birds' and flies away

Source: 2021-04-19 Apple Note. Yahir transcribes a parable his dad told him on 4/18/2021, two weeks before he turned 18. An 18-year-old applies for a job at a circus. The manager mocks: "can you mop the floors?" The kid says: "I can imitate birds." The manager scoffs — until the kid stands up mid-sentence and flies away.

TL;DR

  • Captures a dad parable dated 4/18/2021 — Yahir's father telling him a story about being underestimated on the threshold of adulthood.
  • The 18-year-old's literal claim ("I imitate birds") is taken metaphorically by the manager and missed; the kid demonstrates the literal truth by leaving the conversation airborne.
  • Reads as a latent potential parable: the gatekeeper assumes the applicant is asking permission to mop floors; the applicant was always built for a category the gatekeeper didn't have on the menu.
  • Timing matters — Yahir was literally weeks from 18, weeks from leaving Grants Pass for OSU; dad delivers it as send-off doctrine, not abstract fable.
  • Paired in spirit with the later mentor-from-afar entries — dad is the closest mentor and the parable-source most directly reproduced verbatim in the corpus.

Pattern observed

Sits in the small dad-as-parable-source subset of Apple Notes (the others tend to be food-prep instructions or financial-emergency to-dos). Of those, this one is the most fable-shaped and the most quoted-back. The "fly away" punchline pairs naturally with disconnected from outcome and surface area of luck — the bird doesn't argue with the gatekeeper, it just leaves the floor.

Open questions

  • Did dad invent this or borrow from a source (Spanish-language fable tradition, sermon, etc.)?
  • Has Yahir ever applied this to a specific gatekeeper moment? (Worth checking the Dutch Bros resignation note one month later — 2021 05 04 turkey.)

Related

  • dad wisdom
  • latent potential parable
  • disconnected from outcome
